title: Counters generator (two-colour + plain)
context: Counters are among the simplest and most widely used manipulatives for early number sense, addition, subtraction, and multiplication arrays. Two-colour counters (red/yellow or equivalent colour-blind-safe pair) let learners model signed numbers and two-group problems. Plain counters serve as generic counting tokens. Both variants must pass the verification harness and work in grayscale. This is the first non-fraction-tile generator built on the parametric engine.
objective: Implement a counters generator module for the parametric generator engine that produces two-colour and plain counter sheets in all standard variants (paper sizes, colour modes, standard/large-print).
acceptanceCriteria: Generates counter sheets for two-colour (colour-blind-safe pair, e.g. Okabe-Ito orange and blue) and plain variants.
Output PDFs pass the automated verification harness: counter diameters within tolerance; no colour-only encoding (colour and shape/pattern distinguish the two sides in the two-colour variant).
Generates all standard variants: A4/Letter x colour/grayscale/ink-saver x standard/large-print.
Counters per sheet is configurable via the spec; default sheet fills the printable area at a specified diameter.
MIT-licensed; all commits DCO-signed; passes pnpm build, pnpm test, and CI verification gate.
resources: tasks/math-manip-code-001.json -- parametric generator engine to build on
output: A counters generator module integrated into the generator engine package, with a spec file (specs/counters.spec.json) and example output PDFs, in a pull request, MIT-licensed.
